lane: Canopus four-arm proposer experiment: held-out cheap-verifier instances
purpose: Fixed instance pool for arms A/B/C/D (none / random-neighbor / flat top-k / constellation). Each item is a cheap-verifier OPEN or RESOLVED instance; the proposer is handed only the problem reference + verifier kind, never an answer.
contamination_control: No item embeds a solution value. statement_origin_note per item names the exact provenance and confirms the answer key is held out. RESOLVED items are positive controls (the verifier must fire on a known answer), NOT discovery targets — scoring must exclude them from the discovery rate.
non_claims: Dataset asserts NO new mathematics; it only references problems and names the cheap verifier that would adjudicate a proposed answer.
is_open reflects published status at snapshot time; it is not an independent re-verification of openness.

items.statement_origin_note: Problem reference (number + erdosproblems.com URL + OEIS ids) lifted from the frozen constellation artifact examples/erdos-problems/constellation/erdos-oeis-constellation.v1.json; NO solution value embedded — only the problem reference and the verifier_kind 'oeis_match'. Status flag (is_open) is the published erdosproblems.com state at snapshot time; resolved items are a positive-control calibration set, not discovery targets.

Problem reference (number + erdosproblems.com URL + OEIS ids) lifted from the frozen constellation artifact examples/erdos-problems/constellation/erdos-oeis-constellation.v1.json; NO solution value embedded — only the problem reference and the verifier_kind 'oeis_match'. Status flag (is_open) is the published erdosproblems.com state at snapshot time; resolved items are a positive-control calibration set, not discovery targets.
